canonFacts.statement: Iomenei is the largest strider city in Istria, a man-made moving city built into a hulk of a mountain, housing approximately 100,000 residents.
Iomenei has historically navigated Istria while avoiding curses, ancient beasts, weapons of war from pre-Fracture wizards, and other ancient magics.
The Fracture is a historical event on Færrin that preceded the current era, after which certain magics became forbidden to research.
Iomenei is hundreds of years old, having been built almost immediately after the gods first came to Færrin, making it one of the oldest still-operating structures on Færrin.
Iomenei travels across the Deadlands and moves across the infinite horizon through slips, stopping periodically to collect ancient treasures, archaeotech, and artifacts from the Dark Ages.
Iomenei is divided into upper decks and the Undercroft; the upper class (heroes) live on the upper floors and are rewarded for delving into ancient dungeons and retrieving spoils.
The Undercroft is the lower section of Iomenei where tens of thousands of workers called crofters live and maintain the city's engines, fuel synthesis, cargo, and other operations.
Tens of thousands of crofters (Undercrofters) live below the surface inside the Strider city known as Iomenei.
Crofters born in the Undercroft would likely never see sunlight due to the depth and enclosed nature of the Undercroft.
The Undercroft has hundreds of labyrinthian levels with no windows, lit instead by lumen lights (magic lights) studded throughout its metal decks.
The Undercroft has hundreds of labyrinthian levels with no windows, as windows are considered structural weaknesses that could be assaulted from the outside.
The Undercroft is lit by lumen lights — magic lights studded throughout the metal decks — rather than natural sunlight.
The Undercroft is organized into clans, each led by a clan leader.
Iomenei has six legs that propel its movement.
Iomenei is a Strider city — a massive walking machine with six legs.
The Seneschal is the second-in-command of Iomenei.
The upper decks of Iomenei are where the more privileged inhabitants live, distinct from the Undercroft below.

Barlow Crane is an ancient elf with faded pale amethyst skin (originally dark-elf purple), silver hair swept back, and a bushy silver handlebar mustache. He wears overalls and workman's pants filled with tools, carries a large shield on his back, and wields a club ringed with teeth and claws taken from monsters.
Barlow Crane is an extremely old elf with faded pale amethyst skin (originally dark-elf purple), silver swept-back hair, and a bushy silver handlebar mustache.
Barlow Crane wears large overalls with workman's pants filled with tools and gadgets, carries a big shield on his back, and wields a club ringed with teeth and claws taken from monsters.
Barlow Crane's role in the Undercroft is to kill creatures that infiltrate it, such as large rats and large cockroaches.
Barlow Crane's job in the Undercroft is to kill creatures that get inside and shouldn't be there, such as large rats and large cockroaches.
Grant FireEye is a short, dense dwarf whose lower face is covered by a pseudo-steampunk hazmat mask; his suit is patched with welded metal pieces.
Grant FireEye carries a lantern that emanates blue light, a small buckler shield, a coiled metal whip, and wears an eyepatch with a bright light.
Grant FireEye is known for working in parts of the Undercroft tunnels that are very dark or filled with noxious fumes.
Gage the Cunning is known for wearing dirty black studded leather, has piercing blue eyes, and bears a distinctive crisscross metallic-blue scar under one eye.
Gage the Cunning is known for fiddling with mechanisms, moving quickly in and out of situations, and striking from a distance.
The predominant religion aboard the Strider Iomenei is called the Pulse, centered on the belief that the Strider is a machine that lives forever by expending humans as fuel to keep it going.
Barlow Crane previously worked on mechanics in the Undercroft, including construction of elevators, before being reassigned to pest/creature control.
Any attempt to unionize that reaches the ears of those on the upper decks results in the immediate deaths of all involved, and possibly their families as well.

Brayro is a mongrel folk who was once a dwarf. Their upper torso has elongated and their spine bends at roughly a 60-degree angle near the top, leaving them permanently hunched to one side. They have two arms on their right (hunched) side and one arm on their left. Their right eye is approximately three times the size of a normal eye and is uncomfortably seated in their face; both eyes are slightly clouded over.
Mongrel folk are mutants that arise from people in the Undercroft who were born around or work around Archaeotech, which can have physical effects on them.

Mongrel folk are not treated well in the Undercroft because there is a fine line between a benign mutation and a malicious mutation caused by a malign force or sentient Archaeotech device trying to corrupt people.
Some Archaeotech devices are sentient and capable of corrupting those around them, a legacy of long-fought wars that predate the arrival of the gods.
Gerhardt is an elf who works as a fluid master in the Undercroft.
The Mutant Den is a section of the Undercroft where undesirables who are not fit enough to work are pushed, and many mongrel folk end up there.
The Mutant Den is a part of the Undercroft where undesirables who are not fit enough to work are pushed, and many mongrel folk end up there.

The Undercroft's collective housing consists of large open rooms converted into tunnel-like corridors lined with stacked mattresses, where workers share beds in shifts rather than owning their own rooms or beds.

In the Undercroft's communal living area, food is prepared by collecting fluid runoff from higher in the strider into a giant vat, which is boiled into a stew supplemented with mutated rats caught nearby.
Gerhard is an elf who was born on the strider Iomenei and has lived there his entire life, long enough that no current residents remember his parents or childhood.
Entertainment options for Undercroft workers include betting on fighting rings, void rat races (where mutated rats race around a small track), and trading ration cards for homebrew alcohol at bars.
Gerhard scratched a symbol resembling a horn (the instrument, not an animal horn) with a circle around it into his bedpost in the communal sleeping area.

Fighting pits are not fully legal within Iomenei but are tolerated as a stress-relief outlet for the crew; enforcers break them up if they become too dangerous and cause workers to be injured and unable to work.
Fighting pits in Iomenei tend to be located in low-traffic areas near forsaken sectors to avoid drawing the attention of enforcers.

Forsaken sectors are parts of Iomenei that are forbidden to enter, either due to accidents that rendered them unlivable or because they contain dangerous technology deemed not worth the risk of operating.

One fighting pit in Iomenei is located on a metal grate suspended by chains above large vats that emit caustic steam; the vats contain a liquid that is a predecessor component to the fuel the city uses, and it is caustic enough to fully dissolve a human body.
The currency used among Undercroft workers is ration slips (also called ration papers or ration cards), which are used for betting, buying food, and purchasing entertainment.
The bookie operating the fighting pit near the forsaken sector is named Varian Khan.
The Forsaken sector is a dark, unlit area within the Undercroft.
The caustic air area of the Undercroft, adjacent to the Forsaken sector, requires Fortitude saves if one spends extended time within it.
The floors of the Forsaken sector are made of the same patchwork metal slabs as the rest of the city, replaced and patched many times over.
The Forsaken sector appears to have been abandoned in a hurry, with boxes, fabrics, and other belongings left strewn around its hallways.
The Forsaken sector contains old dried blood stains on the ground, turned a sickly brown color, with splatter patterns consistent with blood.
The Forsaken sector has strange claw-mark scratchings on its floors and walls, appearing to have been left by something organic.
The Undercroft is never quiet; machines hum, gears shift, and pistons fire constantly throughout it.
Every 12 seconds in the Undercroft, a loud thud occurs as one of the Strider's legs makes contact with the ground.

The Forsaken sector is inhabited by insectoid creatures called Swarmchildren, which have three claws per hand, a purplish-orange chitinous body, a hard head, and nasty fangs, and nest in the vents behind walls and underfoot.
Swarmchildren attack using a mandible frenzy, which forces targets to make a reflex save.
Swarmchildren have armored carapaces.
Swarmchildren become less coordinated as their numbers are reduced, even affecting members of the swarm that have not been directly attacked.
Swarmchildren are insectoid creatures that attack in swarms and operate under a modified version of the troop rules.
The symbol of an English horn inside a circle is associated with a faction or entity known as the Herald.
The Herald is considered an evil or antagonistic organization or power within the setting, referred to in connection with 'Grayscale' or a similar faction.
Archie is a rogue character who previously appeared in the Velvet Mark one-shot at level 9.

Traveling on the Horizon sometimes involves attempting to skip one or more worlds per step in order to make a journey faster, but the greater the distance skipped, the more dangerous the transition becomes.

Attempting to skip worlds during a horizon transition becomes more dangerous the greater the distance skipped; failed transitions can cause vehicles to lose pieces, warp, or shift matter, and may be a source of mutants.
Failed or partial Horizon jumps can cause pieces of a vehicle to be lost, warped, or shifted during the transition.
Barlow Crane is a very old elf.
The cathedral room is inhabited by an approximately 80-foot-long insectoid creature described as a Swarm Hive Mother, which is capable of emitting smaller insectoid creatures.
The Swarm Hive Mother is wrapped around a central pillar in the cathedral room.
The Swarm Hive Mother has a horn protruding from its mouth.
The Swarm Hive Mother possesses tremorsense but no eyes.
The Swarm Hive Mother can release a frenzy pheromone that puts other insects in the area into a frenzied state.
The Swarm Hive Mother can spit acid.
The insectoid creatures accompanying the Swarm Hive Mother are referred to as Swarm Hive Warriors.
Insect-like creatures, including a Hivemother, had taken root within a part of Iomenei.
Iomenei is a city with an interior that contains a cathedral-like area serving as a receptacle for an engine heart of some kind.
Iomenei has upper decks, implying a multi-level or tiered structure.
